content:
Prerequisites
Install the below applications

• Node.js v22.17.1

• npm 10.9.2

• Angular CLI 21 (installed using npm install -g @angular/cli)

• Git

• VS Code
Angular CLI requires Node.js, and npm is installed automatically with Node.js.
Angular CLI Version Check
Before creating the project, I verified the installed versions using:
node -v
npm -v
ng versionI noticed that my system still had Angular CLI 8 from my previous work. I upgraded it to Angular CLI 21 before creating the project.
Install Git and its Configuration
Install Git on your local machine and configure your Git username and email using the following commands:
git config --global user.name
git config --global user.emailCreating the Project
ng new angular-employee-managementBelow are options asked while creating a new project:

• Standalone Components

• SCSS

• Routing

• SSR — No ( Major focus is on angular 21 features, so skipping for now)

• AI tool selection — based on user interest, I have selected none for now.
What I completed

• Installed Git

• Configured Git username and email

• Installed Angular Language Service

• Installed ESLint

• Installed Prettier

• Created Angular 21 project

• Initialized Git repository

• Made first commit using Conventional Commits

• Connected GitHub remote

• Resolved my first merge conflict

• Successfully pushed the project to GitHub
